reasoning:
As of April 12, 2026, the Oklahoma City Thunder have successfully secured the #1 seed in the Western Conference for the third straight season and are the defending 2025 NBA Champions. Recent injury reports confirm that their core rotation (SGA, J-Dub, Chet Holmgren, Alex Caruso, Isaiah Hartenstein) is healthy, with the team utilizing the final two regular-season games strictly for rest and "injury management."

Futures markets heavily favor the Thunder to repeat as Western Conference Champions. Odds from major sportsbooks (like BetMGM and FanDuel) range between -140 and -150 to win the West. Adjusting for the standard sportsbook overround/vigorish, the implied true probability is around 54% to 57.5%. Advanced predictive models like Dimers give the Thunder a 53.6% chance, while Polymarket sentiment reflects an actively traded 57% probability. Considering their health, home-court advantage throughout the West playoffs, and historically dominant regular-season profile, setting the probability between 56% and 57% accurately reflects the consensus expert and market expectation prior to the start of the 2026 NBA Playoffs. I've slightly stepped down the probabilities for the later resolution slots to reflect widening uncertainty over longer horizons.
